Exchange login token for bearer token
steps.observed: Received bearer token for ia-r2-cli-94bc@example.com (tier: free)

assertions.title: spikes whoami Command

steps.observed: Error: JSON error: expected value at line 1 column 1

steps.expected: JSON response with email and tier fields
steps.observed: Production endpoint returns HTML landing page (<!DOCTYPE html>) instead of JSON

steps.expected: Error message: 'Not logged in. Run 'spikes login' first.'
steps.observed: Error: IO error: Not logged in. Run 'spikes login' first.

steps.action: Verify local worker /me endpoint works correctly
steps.expected: JSON response with email and tier

outputs: Error: JSON error: expected value at line 1 column 1
evidence.consoleErrors: JSON parse error - production API not fully deployed

assertions.issues: Production API at https://spikes.sh/me returns HTML landing page instead of JSON user info. The local worker at localhost:8787/me works correctly and returns the expected JSON format. The CLI code correctly implements the whoami behavior per the specification - it calls GET /me with Bearer token and parses the JSON response for 'email' and 'tier' fields. The failure is due to production API deployment status, not CLI implementation.

blockers.description: Production API endpoint https://spikes.sh/me returns HTML landing page instead of JSON user info. The CLI correctly implements the expected behavior but cannot complete end-to-end test against production.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Verified local worker at localhost:8787/me returns correct JSON format: {"email":"ia-r2-cli-94bc@example.com","tier":"free"}. CLI code inspection confirms correct implementation - calls GET /me with Authorization: Bearer header and parses JSON response for email and tier fields.

summary: Tested 2 assertions: 1 passed (VAL-AUTH-014), 1 blocked (VAL-AUTH-016). VAL-AUTH-014 confirms spikes login --token correctly stores bearer token in platform-appropriate config directory (~/Library/Application Support/spikes/auth.toml on macOS) with 0600 permissions. VAL-AUTH-016 is blocked due to production API returning HTML instead of JSON; the CLI implementation is correct per specification and local worker testing confirms the expected behavior works correctly.
